questions.question: Explain the difference between rational and irrational numbers with examples.
questions.answer: Rational numbers can be written in the form p/q where q ≠ 0, such as 1/2 or 3/4. Irrational numbers cannot be expressed as p/q and have non-terminating non-repeating decimals such as √2 and π.

questions.question: Explain the classification of real numbers with a diagram.
questions.answer: Real numbers consist of rational and irrational numbers. Rational numbers include integers and fractions. Irrational numbers cannot be written as fractions.

questions.question: Explain terminating and non-terminating decimals with examples.
questions.answer: Terminating decimals end after a finite number of digits such as 0.25. Non-terminating decimals continue infinitely and may be repeating or non-repeating.

questions.question: Explain why √16 is rational and √2 is irrational.
questions.answer: √16 = 4 which is an integer and therefore rational. √2 cannot be written as p/q and its decimal expansion is non-terminating non-repeating.

questions.question: Explain the concept of number line representation of real numbers.
questions.answer: Real numbers can be represented as points on a number line where each point corresponds to a real number.

questions.question: Explain prime numbers with examples.
questions.answer: Prime numbers have exactly two factors: 1 and the number itself. Examples include 2, 3, and 5.

questions.question: Explain composite numbers with examples.
questions.answer: Composite numbers have more than two factors. Examples include 4, 6 and 8.

questions.question: Explain coprime numbers with examples.
questions.answer: Two numbers are coprime if their HCF is 1. Example: 8 and 15.

questions.question: Explain the concept of square numbers.
questions.answer: Square numbers are obtained when a number is multiplied by itself such as 4, 9 and 16.

questions.question: Explain the concept of square roots.
questions.answer: The square root of a number is a value that when multiplied by itself gives the original number.

questions.question: Explain why π is irrational.
questions.answer: π has a non-terminating non-repeating decimal expansion and cannot be written as a fraction.

questions.question: Explain why √25 is rational.
questions.answer: √25 = 5 which is an integer and therefore rational.

questions.question: Explain the decimal expansion of rational numbers.
questions.answer: The decimal expansion of rational numbers either terminates or repeats infinitely.

questions.question: Explain irrational numbers with examples.
questions.answer: Irrational numbers cannot be written as fractions and have non-terminating non-repeating decimals.

questions.question: Explain the importance of prime numbers.
questions.answer: Prime numbers are building blocks of natural numbers through prime factorization.

questions.question: Explain the concept of factors and multiples.
questions.answer: Factors divide numbers exactly while multiples are obtained by multiplying a number.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of perfect squares.
questions.answer: Perfect squares are numbers whose square roots are integers.

questions.question: Explain why 0 is a rational number.
questions.answer: 0 can be written as 0/1 where numerator and denominator are integers.

questions.question: Explain the role of irrational numbers in mathematics.
questions.answer: Irrational numbers help represent values like π and √2 used in geometry and measurement.

questions.question: Explain why every integer is a rational number.
questions.answer: Any integer can be written as p/q where q=1.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of decimal representation of numbers.
questions.answer: Decimal representation expresses numbers using digits and decimal point.

questions.question: Explain why √1 is rational.
questions.answer: √1 = 1 which is an integer.

questions.question: Explain why √3 is irrational.
questions.answer: √3 cannot be written as p/q and its decimal expansion is non-terminating non-repeating.

questions.question: Explain the difference between factors and prime factors.
questions.answer: Factors divide numbers exactly while prime factors are prime numbers that divide the number.

questions.question: Explain the concept of real numbers on number line.
questions.answer: Every real number corresponds to a point on the number line.
